notes: earthworks in full swing today on the subdivision. pad prep for lots 14 through 20
running 2 excavators: the 30 tonne Hitachi ZX300 on the main cut and the 14 tonne CAT 314 on the service trenches. both started at 6am
also got the D6 dozer pushing material from the cut to the fill areas on the western lots. Thommo operating

8 truck and dogs running today carting fill from the borrow pit on Smiths farm. each one carries about 16 cubic metres so thats 128 cubes per round trip across all trucks. theyre averaging 4 trips each today so roughly 500 cubic metres of fill delivered
the fill material is a sandy clay, tested and approved by the geotech last week. moisture content is pretty close to optimum which is good given we havent had rain for a week
the 18 tonne padfoot roller is doing the compaction on lot 14. 4 passes at 300mm layers as per the spec. Dazzas been on the roller since 7
geotech is on site doing density tests. hes done 3 tests so far on lot 14: test 1 was 96% MDD, test 2 was 98%, test 3 was 97%. all pass the 95% minimum. nice one
the water cart has been going non stop keeping the dust down and conditioning the fill. 10,000 litre cart, Kevvo driving. hes refilled it twice already today from the hydrant on the corner

fuel truck came at lunch time. filled up the 30 tonne excavator (350 litres), the 14 tonne (150 litres), the dozer (200 litres), and topped up the roller (80 litres). also filled the water cart. total diesel delivery was about 900 litres
we've moved approximately 800 cubic metres of material today between the cut, fill, and imported material. surveyor checked the levels on lot 14 and 15, both within 20mm of design which is spot on
the grader came on this afternoon for 2 hours to trim the road formation on the new internal road. operator Johno has a good eye, got it looking perfectly flat. the road base goes on next week

end of day equipment summary: Hitachi 30t excavator 9 hours, CAT 14t excavator 8 hours, D6 dozer 8 hours, padfoot roller 7 hours, grader 2 hours, water cart 9 hours. 8 tip trucks averaging 7 hours each. no breakdowns today which is a bloody miracle on this job
materials consumed: approximately 500 cubic metres of imported fill, about 900 litres of diesel, and probably 30,000 litres of water between dust suppression and compaction conditioning
surveyor pegged out lots 16 and 17 ready for tomorrow. the cut on lot 16 is gonna be about 1.5m deep so a fair bit of material to shift. dozer and the 30 tonner will handle it
